courseTitle: B2 TED TALKS第5堂 - 壞習慣傷大腦！Bad Habits That Raise Your Risk for Dementia

vocabulary.definition: a general term for severe decline in memory and thinking ability
vocabulary.example: Every three seconds someone in the world develops dementia.

vocabulary.definition: the condition of being severely overweight
vocabulary.example: Sitting too long increases your risk for obesity, heart disease, and diabetes.

vocabulary.definition: a disease where the body cannot properly control blood sugar levels
vocabulary.example: Sitting for long periods can raise your risk for diabetes.

vocabulary.definition: the practice of keeping the mouth clean and free of disease
vocabulary.example: Poor oral hygiene is also a factor in dementia risk.

vocabulary.definition: very bad or intense in degree
vocabulary.example: Severe tooth loss increased dementia risk six times.

vocabulary.definition: something that provides access to another thing; an entry point
vocabulary.example: The mouth is the gateway to the rest of the body.

vocabulary.definition: the state of being separated from other people
vocabulary.example: The effect of social isolation on our health is as powerful as smoking.

vocabulary.definition: the feeling of being alone and unhappy about it
vocabulary.example: Social isolation and loneliness are as dangerous as smoking or high blood pressure.

vocabulary.definition: avoiding excess; doing things in reasonable amounts
vocabulary.example: Drink in moderation to keep your brain healthy.

vocabulary.definition: a substance injected to provide immunity against a disease
vocabulary.example: Skipping vaccines could also increase your dementia risk.

vocabulary.definition: the most common form of dementia, causing memory loss and cognitive decline
vocabulary.example: Getting a flu shot lowered their risk for Alzheimer's.

vocabulary.definition: a serious infection of the lungs
vocabulary.example: Those who got the pneumonia vaccine were 30% less likely to develop dementia.

vocabulary.definition: informal: in perfect condition; functioning at its best
vocabulary.example: Call a friend to keep your brain in tip-top shape.
So get some shut-eye, drink in moderation, and call a friend.

sentences.sentence: Every three seconds someone in the world develops dementia.
sentences.zh: 全球每三秒就有一人罹患失智症。

sentences.sentence: Sitting for at least 12 hours a day increases your risk by 63 percent.
sentences.zh: 每天久坐至少 12 小時，罹患失智症的風險增加 63%。

sentences.sentence: The effect of social isolation and loneliness on our health is as powerful as things like smoking, high blood pressure, obesity.
sentences.zh: 社交孤立與孤獨感對健康的影響，跟吸菸、高血壓、肥胖一樣強烈。

sentences.sentence: So get some shut-eye, drink in moderation, and call a friend to keep your brain in tip-top shape.
sentences.zh: 所以好好睡一覺、適量飲酒、打電話給朋友，讓大腦維持在最佳狀態。

sentences.sentence: The mouth is the gateway to the rest of the body — if we clean our mouths properly, we can have a tremendous effect not only in dentistry but also in overall health.
sentences.zh: 嘴巴是通往全身的門戶——如果能好好清潔口腔，不只對牙齒，對整體健康也會有巨大影響。

grammar.rule: Not just...but also / Not only...but also（不只⋯也⋯）
grammar.explanation: 強調除了一個已知的結果，還有另一個（通常更令人意外的）影響。
examples: Sitting for long periods doesn't just increase your risk for obesity, heart disease, and diabetes, but it can also raise your risk for dementia.
We can have a tremendous effect not only in dentistry but also in overall health.

grammar.rule: As + adjective + as（同等比較：和⋯一樣⋯）
grammar.explanation: 比較兩件事程度相同，表達「A 和 B 一樣⋯」。
examples: The effect of social isolation and loneliness is as powerful as things like smoking, high blood pressure, obesity.
severe tooth loss increased their dementia risk six times more than people who lost fewer teeth.

topicSummary: 這堂課的影片要告訴你：有哪些日常壞習慣，正在悄悄提高你得失智症的風險？全球每三秒就有一人罹患失智症，但很多風險其實可以預防。影片提到幾個讓人驚訝的因素：久坐不動、睡眠不足、口腔衛生差（嚴重掉牙讓失智風險暴增 6 倍！）、社交孤立（跟吸菸一樣危險！），還有少打疫苗。保護大腦，從生活習慣開始。
discussion: 影片提到的壞習慣中，哪一個最讓你驚訝？為什麼？
你自己平常有哪些可能傷害大腦健康的習慣？
「社交孤立跟吸菸一樣危險」——你認同這個說法嗎？你有沒有切身的感受？
如果你今天要改變一個習慣來保護大腦，你會先從哪裡開始？
